behavior_summary: Provides the in-game response when the player examines (looks at) the BOAT object: it displays a short bark text "boat" and then suspends/ends the U8P_BOAT::look process.
There are no branches, menus, external calls, or global flag reads/writes; the behavior is a single-shot descriptive bark.
The presence of a suspend node after the bark indicates the process yields or terminates immediately after presenting the text.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BOAT::look executes as a U8P_BOAT::look-type process (process form) and shows one Bark node with the text "boat".
Execution then hits a suspend/end node, so the process does not continue to other actions or loops.
open_questions: Does the suspend node terminate the process outright or merely yield to the engine for short-lived display timing? (Effect is inferred but not explicit.)
Is the bark text "boat" a literal string or a lookup ID for localized text in the surrounding asset pipeline? (Source is the decompiled bark node; localization mapping is outside this script.)

main_function.name: U8P_BOAT::look
main_function.purpose: Serve as the U8P_BOAT::look/examine handler for the BOAT object: present a brief description/bark and then suspend/finish the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses the game's U8P_BOAT::look/examine interaction on the BOAT (process type 0x0208).
Implicitly triggered by the engine when the BOAT object receives a U8P_BOAT::look event.

function_map.name: U8P_BOAT::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler that emits a single bark text and suspends the process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the in-game response when the player examines (looks at) the BOAT object: it displays a short bark text "boat" and then suspends/ends the U8P_BOAT::look process.
